name: Na Mea Hawaii
tagline: The best shop on Oahu for genuinely Hawaiian-made gifts — koa wood bowls, lauhala weaving, and books by Native Hawaiian authors.
parking: Ward Centre parking (1200 Ala Moana Blvd, Suite 270)
bestTime: Weekday afternoons; check for cultural workshops and author events
talkStory: Founded by Maile Meyer, a Native Hawaiian cultural advocate, Na Mea Hawaii (meaning 'things Hawaiian') is a mission-driven shop supporting Hawaiian artisans and cultural practitioners. Every item -- from hand-carved koa bowls to lauhala weavings, books by Native Hawaiian authors, and Hawaiian-language children's books -- is authentically made. Meyer hosts regular workshops in lauhala weaving and lei making. This is the antidote to Waikiki's made-in-China souvenir shops.
